app.title: Election Process Assistant
app.disclaimer: Educational summary only. Verify every detail with the Election Commission of India and the Chief Electoral Officer for your state.
app.sourcesToggle: Open or close official sources for this step

errors.parse_failed: Content could not be loaded. Try refreshing the page.
errors.network: Could not reach the server. Check your connection and try again.

schedule.title: Election schedule and Model Code of Conduct
schedule.bodyVoter: The Election Commission announces the poll schedule. The Model Code of Conduct sets norms for parties, candidates, and government during the election period.
schedule.bodyCandidate: After notification, nominations open on published dates. The Model Code of Conduct applies to campaigning and day-to-day conduct.
schedule.bodyObserver: Observers track compliance with ECI instructions and the Model Code of Conduct during the process.

nominations.title: Filing of nominations
nominations.bodyVoter: Candidates file nomination papers with the Returning Officer within the prescribed window.
nominations.bodyCandidate: You submit your nomination during the notified period and follow statutory forms and deposits as per ECI handbooks.
nominations.bodyObserver: Nomination filing follows a fixed timetable published by the Returning Officer.

scrutiny.title: Scrutiny of nominations
scrutiny.bodyVoter: The Returning Officer examines whether nomination papers are valid under law.
scrutiny.bodyCandidate: Your papers may be accepted or rejected based on scrutiny outcomes; timelines are fixed by law and ECI directions.
scrutiny.bodyObserver: Scrutiny is conducted openly as per ECI procedures.

withdrawal.title: Withdrawal of candidatures
withdrawal.bodyVoter: Candidates may withdraw within the notified window, finalizing the contest list.
withdrawal.bodyCandidate: You may withdraw candidature before the deadline if you choose not to contest.
withdrawal.bodyObserver: Withdrawal deadlines are published and strictly enforced.

campaign.title: Campaign period
campaign.bodyVoter: Parties and candidates campaign until 48 hours before polling ends (silence period rules apply as per ECI).
campaign.bodyCandidate: You campaign within expenditure limits and conduct rules set by the ECI.
campaign.bodyObserver: Observers note adherence to expenditure and conduct norms.

polling.title: Polling day
polling.bodyVoter: Carry your EPIC or permitted ID, know your polling station, and follow queue and EVM+VVPAT procedures as guided by polling staff.
polling.bodyCandidate: You follow ECI rules near polling stations; agents are appointed as per law.
polling.bodyObserver: Observers monitor polling processes as authorized by the ECI.

counting.title: Counting of votes
counting.bodyVoter: Votes are counted under ECI supervision with prescribed transparency measures.
counting.bodyCandidate: Counting follows ECI guidelines; results are declared for each constituency.
counting.bodyObserver: Observers witness counting arrangements as per ECI instructions.

results.title: Declaration of results
results.bodyVoter: Winners are declared; NOTA tallies appear as per rules. Use official ECI channels for certified outcomes.
results.bodyCandidate: Results are published officially; further remedies follow election law.
results.bodyObserver: Result declarations are documented and communicated through official channels.

nota.definition: None Of The Above — an option on the EVM to register no preference among candidates.

constituency.definition: A territorial area that elects one member to Lok Sabha under the delimitation in force.

epic.definition: Electors Photo Identity Card — commonly used ID to vote when listed in the electoral roll.

ro.definition: Officer who receives nominations, conducts scrutiny, and supervises polling and counting for a constituency.

nomination.definition: Formal submission of candidature within the notified period before scrutiny.

scrutiny.definition: Legal examination of nomination papers for validity.

mcc.definition: ECI guidelines on conduct for parties, candidates, and government during elections.

evm.definition: Electronic Voting Machine used for recording votes in most constituencies.

vvpat.definition: Voter Verifiable Paper Audit Trail — slip showing symbol for verification before the vote is stored.

counting.definition: Official tally of valid votes under ECI supervision.

consent.title: Optional analytics (India DPDPA)
consent.body: We use Firebase Analytics only if you accept, to understand how features are used. App Check and hosting security always apply. You can decline analytics and still use the app.

locator.title: ECI office map
locator.lede: Find the Election Commission of India headquarters on Google Maps (embed). Configure a Maps Embed API key in firebase-public.json to enable the map.
locator.missingKey: Maps Embed API key is not configured. Add mapsEmbedApiKey for this section.
locator.iframeTitle: Google Map of Election Commission of India office
locator.disclaimer: For polling-station lookup, always use your state CEO or the official ECI voter services channels.

assistant.title: Ask about the election process
assistant.lede: Educational answers are generated with Google Gemini via Cloud Functions. Always verify facts on eci.gov.in.
assistant.unconfigured: Firebase is not configured in this build, so cloud answers are unavailable.
assistant.placeholder: Example: What does a Returning Officer do?
assistant.helper: Ask one clear question at a time for the best answer quality.
assistant.errorGeneric: Could not reach the assistant. Try again later.
assistant.emptyState: Your answer appears here after you ask a question.

suggestions.pollingDay: What should a voter carry on polling day?
suggestions.nota: What does NOTA mean in Lok Sabha elections?
suggestions.returningOfficer: What are the duties of a Returning Officer?
suggestions.counting: How are votes counted and results declared?

glossaryUi.cloudTranslate: Improve Hindi with Google Cloud Translation
glossaryUi.cloudHint: Uses Cloud Translation API via Firebase Functions when Hindi is active.

timelineUi.exportError: Export failed. Check Cloud Functions and Sheets service account secret.
timelineUi.rajyaPreview: Preview: Remote Config indicates upcoming Rajya Sabha content may be enabled.
